name: Educate users for unused features
description: Detect when users haven’t tried an important feature yet and send a helpful tip encouraging them to explore it. In this workflow, we're using PostHog Workflows as an example.

actions.name: User signs up
actions.description: User performs an action to start the workflow.

actions.name: Has used feature?
actions.description: Select the usage event for your desired feature.

actions.name: Usage tip 1
actions.description: Send a feature tip to the user.

value.html:
One small workflow can save a lot of manual work

Quick tip: the best workflows are usually the simplest ones.

 

Many teams start with small automations like:

 

• alerting the team when something fails
• sending a message when a user signs up
• notifying support when someone leaves negative feedback

 

They’re easy to set up and quietly remove a bunch of repetitive work.

Might be worth trying one.
Get started
 

p.s. This email was sent with PostHog Workflows! 🦔

Boring, legal bit: We're at 2261 Market Street #4008, CA 94114. Unsubscribe?

value.html:
Make your workflows smarter

A small trick that makes workflows much more useful: add conditions.

 

Instead of doing the same thing every time, you can branch based on what users actually do.

 

For example:

 

• if a payment fails → notify the team
• if a user signs up → send onboarding email
• if someone reaches a milestone → celebrate it

 

A bit of logic goes a long way.
Get started with Workflows
 

p.s. This email was sent with PostHog Workflows! 🦔

Boring, legal bit: We're at 2261 Market Street #4008, CA 94114. Unsubscribe?
